methodology: Started from the current known page ranks in the two public ranking CSVs, then scored every row one by one for June 8, 2026 alpha-ranking relevance.

Move decisions prioritize hidden or under-owned upside adjusted for directness to CPO, HBM, advanced packaging, substrates/materials, test/probe/metrology, memory, AI networking and 2027 AI compute demand.

Current primary-source evidence received the most weight: NVIDIA CPO production, Broadcom and Marvell AI networking/custom silicon disclosures, memory supplier results, and recent packaging/test/metrology orders.

Large market caps, high price-to-sales or known crowding, and large 2026 YTD reratings cap or reverse otherwise valid thematic exposure; non-US market-cap values from the source CSV are treated as directional when the CSV itself flags verification risk.

rankMove means relative page relevance: up = should rank better, hold = current rank still defensible, down = lower priority, remove = no longer actionable enough for the alpha ranking, add = reserved for unranked names and not used because this task covered existing page stocks.
